intro: Trichantha serrata, also known as Wild Sarsaparilla, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Annonaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Trichantha serrata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Trichantha serrata is often prepared as an infusion or a decoction, and is commonly used in the form of syrups, tinctures, and salves, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects.
